# smolanalytics

> Self-hosted product + web analytics in one Go binary. Deterministic reports (funnels,
> retention, trends, paths, web traffic) exposed over HTTP and MCP, so AI assistants
> answer analytics questions with exact computed numbers — never generated SQL.

## Install (for agents adding analytics to an app)

Run the server (pick one):
- docker: `docker run -d -p 8080:8080 -v sa_data:/data -e SMOLANALYTICS_DB=/data/events.log -e SMOLANALYTICS_WRITE_KEY=<key> ghcr.io/arjun0606/smolanalytics:latest serve`
- binary: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Arjun0606/smolanalytics/main/install.sh | sh`

Web app — add to the HTML head (autocaptures pageviews + clicks, SPA-aware):
```html
<script src="https://YOUR_HOST/sdk.js"></script>
<script>smolanalytics.init("YOUR_WRITE_KEY", { host: "https://YOUR_HOST" });</script>
```
Cookieless (no consent banner needed): `smolanalytics.init(key, { host, anonymous: true })`.
On login: `smolanalytics.identify(userId)`. Custom events: `smolanalytics.track("signup", { plan: "pro" })`.

Backend/mobile — POST one endpoint, any language:
```
POST https://YOUR_HOST/v1/events
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_WRITE_KEY
{ "name": "signup", "distinct_id": "user_123", "properties": { "plan": "pro" } }
```
Batch = JSON array (up to 10k events). Use a stable distinct_id per user.

## Connect an AI assistant (MCP)

`smolanalytics connect` wires Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor / Windsurf / VS Code / Cline
automatically. Manual stdio config: command `smolanalytics`, args `["mcp"]`, env
`SMOLANALYTICS_DB=<path>`. Remote: Streamable HTTP at `POST https://YOUR_HOST/mcp` with
`Authorization: Bearer <write key>`. 43 tools + 3 prompts. Read (overview, funnel,
retention, trends, breakdown, web_overview, paths, lifecycle, stickiness, whats_notable, …),
act (create_alert, add_webhook, create_cohort, save_report + list/delete), and control the
instance (set_project, set_retention, create/revoke API keys).

## For agents wiring analytics into an app (the intended loop)

1. Install tracking (snippet or POST — above), with a stable distinct_id per user.
2. Declare intent: `set_tracking_plan` with every event + expected property keys.
3. After the app runs, verify: `instrumentation_health` reports per planned event —
   flowing / MISSING / missing_properties — plus unplanned events. Iterate until healthy.
4. Set a drop alert on the most important event (`create_alert`) and a webhook
   (`add_webhook`) so regressions surface without anyone checking a dashboard.
The `instrument-my-app` MCP prompt walks this exact loop.

## Key facts
- Reports are deterministic — tool answers always equal dashboard numbers (same engine).
- Unknown event/property names return corrective errors listing valid names (never silent zeros).
- One static binary, zero dependencies, MIT. Data stays on your infrastructure.
- Docs: https://github.com/Arjun0606/smolanalytics (framework guides in /docs).
- Hosted version: https://smolanalytics-site.vercel.app (cloud, coming soon).
